review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls relevant to change management. Audit logging and retention are crucial for tracking changes, and admission controllers can enforce change management policies. RBAC roles are essential for defining who can make changes. However, the naming of audit-related functions can be improved for clarity and specificity regarding their role in change management.
naming_improvements: Use 'change_tracking' in audit function names to specify their role in change management
Ensure function names clearly indicate their purpose related to change management
title: Change Management Process
step1_reasoning: The control 'Change Management Process' in cloud environments often involves tracking changes through audit logs and ensuring configurations are monitored and managed. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring audit logging is enabled and retained for tracking changes. Additionally, admission controllers can enforce change management policies by validating configurations before they are applied. RBAC roles can be defined to manage who can make changes, aligning with change management processes.
